scenarios.title: Damage due to unauthorized third-party repair
scenarios.description: The iPhone experienced hardware failure after repair by a non-Apple authorized service provider.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to damage caused by service performed by anyone who is not a representative of Apple or an Apple Authorized Service Provider.

scenarios.reasoning: Damage resulting from unauthorized repair is excluded under section 4.2(ii)(d). Therefore, no coverage or service fee applies.

scenarios.title: Damage from Operating Outside Manufacturer's Intended Use
scenarios.description: User subjected the iPhone to high temperature environment causing internal component failure.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to damage caused by operating the Covered iPhone outside the permitted or intended uses described by the manufacturer.

scenarios.reasoning: The damage resulted from operating the device outside of intended environmental parameters, which is excluded under section 4.2(ii)(c). No service fee applies as the claim is denied.

scenarios.title: Damage from Modified iPhone Serial Number
scenarios.description: Customer submits a claim for hardware failure on an iPhone whose serial number has been altered after purchase.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to an iPhone with a serial number that has been altered, defaced or removed, or has been modified to alter its functionality or capability without the written permission of the manufacturer.

scenarios.reasoning: The claim is denied because the Covered iPhone’s serial number was altered, which is an explicit exclusion under section 4.2(iii). Such modification voids coverage and service fees do not apply.

scenarios.title: Claim for pre-existing battery degradation purchased after Plan
scenarios.description: Customer submits a claim for battery capacity loss exceeding 50%, but the degradation was known and existed before Plan purchase.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to a pre-existing condition on any Covered iPhone if you purchased the Plan after you purchased the Covered iPhone.

scenarios.reasoning: The claim involves a battery defect that was pre-existing prior to Plan purchase, which is excluded from coverage under section 4.2(viii). No service fee applies since no repair or replacement is authorized.
